is a [mobile operating system](/wiki/Mobile_operating_system "Mobile operating system") developed by [Apple](/wiki/Apple_Inc. "Apple Inc.") for its [iPad](/wiki/IPad "IPad") line of [tablet computers](/wiki/Tablet_computer "Tablet computer"). It was given a name distinct from [iOS](/wiki/IOS "IOS"), the operating system used by Apple's [iPhones](/wiki/IPhone "IPhone"), to reflect the diverging features of the two product lines, such as multitasking. It was introduced as [iPadOS 13](/wiki/IPadOS_13 "IPadOS 13"), reflecting its status as the successor to [iOS 12](/wiki/IOS_12 "IOS 12") for the iPad, and first released to the public on September 24, 2019.[[7]](#cite_note-AppleInsider-7) [Major versions of iPadOS](/wiki/IPadOS_version_history "IPadOS version history") are released annually; the current stable version, [iPadOS 26.4](/wiki/IPadOS_26 "IPadOS 26"), was released to the public on March 24, 2026 (2026-03-24).

**iPadOS** is a [mobile operating system](/wiki/Mobile_operating_system "Mobile operating system") developed by [Apple](/wiki/Apple_Inc. "Apple Inc.") for its [iPad](/wiki/IPad "IPad") line of [tablet computers](/wiki/Tablet_computer "Tablet computer"). It was given a name distinct from [iOS](/wiki/IOS "IOS"), the operating system used by Apple's [iPhones](/wiki/IPhone "IPhone"), to reflect the diverging features of the two product lines, such as multitasking. It was introduced as [iPadOS 13](/wiki/IPadOS_13 "IPadOS 13"), reflecting its status as the successor to [iOS 12](/wiki/IOS_12 "IOS 12") for the iPad, and first released to the public on September 24, 2019.[[7]](#cite_note-AppleInsider-7) [Major versions of iPadOS](/wiki/IPadOS_version_history "IPadOS version history") are released annually; the current stable version, [iPadOS 26.4](/wiki/IPadOS_26 "IPadOS 26"), was released to the public on March 24, 2026 (2026-03-24).

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=IPadOS&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

See also: [iPadOS version history](/wiki/IPadOS_version_history "IPadOS version history")

The [first iPad](/wiki/IPad_(1st_generation) "IPad (1st generation)") was introduced on April 3, 2010, and ran [iPhone OS 3.2](/wiki/IPhone_OS_3 "IPhone OS 3"), which added support for the larger device to the operating system, previously only used on the [iPhone](/wiki/IPhone "IPhone") and its smaller counterpart, the [iPod touch](/wiki/IPod_touch "IPod touch"). This shared operating system was rebranded as [iOS](/wiki/IOS "IOS") with the release of [iOS 4](/wiki/IOS_4 "IOS 4")[[8]](#cite_note-ciscosuit-8) in June 2010.

The operating system initially had rough feature parity running on the iPhone, iPod Touch, and iPad, with variations in user interface depending on screen size, and minor differences in the selection of apps included.[[9]](#cite_note-9) However, over time, the variant of iOS for the iPad incorporated a growing set of differentiating features, such as [picture-in-picture](/wiki/Picture-in-picture "Picture-in-picture"), the ability to display multiple running apps [simultaneously](/wiki/Computer_multitasking "Computer multitasking") (both introduced with [iOS 9](/wiki/IOS_9 "IOS 9") in 2015), [drag and drop](/wiki/Drag_and_drop "Drag and drop"), and a dock that more closely resembled the dock from [macOS](/wiki/MacOS "MacOS") than the one on the iPhone (added in 2017 with [iOS 11](/wiki/IOS_11 "IOS 11")). Standard iPad apps were increasingly designed to support the optional use of a physical keyboard.

To emphasize the different feature set available on the iPad, and to signal their intention to develop the platforms in divergent directions, at the [Worldwide Developers Conference](/wiki/Apple_Worldwide_Developers_Conference "Apple Worldwide Developers Conference") (WWDC) 2019, Apple announced that the variant of iOS that runs on the iPad would be rebranded as "iPadOS". The new naming strategy began with [iPadOS 13.1](/wiki/IPadOS_13.1 "IPadOS 13.1"), in 2019.[[7]](#cite_note-AppleInsider-7)

On June 22, 2020, at WWDC 2020, Apple announced [iPadOS 14](/wiki/IPadOS_14 "IPadOS 14"), with compact designs for search, [Siri](/wiki/Siri "Siri"), and calls, improved app designs, handwriting recognition, better AR features, enhanced privacy protections, and app widgets.[[10]](#cite_note-10) iPadOS 14 was released to the public on September 16, 2020.

On June 7, 2021, at WWDC 2021, [iPadOS 15](/wiki/IPadOS_15 "IPadOS 15") was announced with widgets on the Home Screen and App Library, the same features that came to iPhone with iOS 14 in 2020. The update also brought stricter privacy measurements with Safari such as IP Address blocking so other websites cannot see it. iPadOS 15 was released to the public on September 20, 2021.

On June 6, 2022, at WWDC 2022, [iPadOS 16](/wiki/IPadOS_16 "IPadOS 16") was announced with a [Weather](/wiki/Weather_(Apple) "Weather (Apple)") app[[11]](#cite_note-11) and Stage Manager,[[12]](#cite_note-12) along with most of the features included in [iOS 16](/wiki/IOS_16 "IOS 16"), excluding a customizable lock screen.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

On June 5, 2023, at WWDC 2023, Apple announced [iPadOS 17](/wiki/IPadOS_17 "IPadOS 17") with support for widgets for the lock screen, a feature originally launched with iOS 16, along with the majority of features announced included in [iOS 17](/wiki/IOS_17 "IOS 17"). In addition, iPadOS 17 now includes the Apple Health app.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

On June 10, 2024, at WWDC 2024, Apple announced [iPadOS 18](/wiki/IPadOS_18 "IPadOS 18").[[15]](#cite_note-15)

On June 9, 2025, at WWDC 2025, Apple announced [iPadOS 26](/wiki/IPadOS_26 "IPadOS 26").[[16]](#cite_note-16)

## Features

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=IPadOS&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Features")]

Many features of iPadOS are also available on iOS; however, iPadOS contains some features that are not available in iOS and lacks some features that are available in iOS.

### iPadOS 13

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=IPadOS&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: iPadOS 13")]

Further information: [iPadOS 13 § Features](/wiki/IPadOS_13#Features "IPadOS 13")

### iPadOS 14

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=IPadOS&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: iPadOS 14")]

Further information: [iPadOS 14 § Features](/wiki/IPadOS_14#Features "IPadOS 14")

#### Scribble

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=IPadOS&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Scribble")]

Introduced in iPadOS 14, Scribble converts text handwritten by an [Apple Pencil](/wiki/Apple_Pencil "Apple Pencil") into typed text in most text fields.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

### iPadOS 15

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=IPadOS&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: iPadOS 15")]

Further information: [iPadOS 15 § Features](/wiki/IPadOS_15#Features "IPadOS 15")

#### Widgets

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=IPadOS&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Widgets")]

Beginning with iPadOS 15, widgets can be placed on the home screen.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

#### Translate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=IPadOS&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Translate")]

Beginning with iPadOS 15, Translate is available. The feature was announced on June 7, 2021, at WWDC 2021. Translation works with 11 languages.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

### iPadOS 16

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=IPadOS&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: iPadOS 16")]

Further information: [iPadOS 16 § Features](/wiki/IPadOS_16#Features "IPadOS 16")

#### Weather

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=IPadOS&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Weather")]

Beginning with iPadOS 16, the Weather app was added to iPad. The application had previously only been available on the iPhone and iPod Touch.[[19]](#cite_note-19) The feature was announced on June 6, 2022, at WWDC 2022.

#### Stage Manager

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=IPadOS&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Stage Manager")]

iPadOS 16 adds a new feature called Stage Manager that automatically sorts windows by app.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

### iPadOS 17

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=IPadOS&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: iPadOS 17")]

Further information: [iPadOS 17 § Features](/wiki/IPadOS_17#Features "IPadOS 17")

#### Lock Screen

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=IPadOS&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Lock Screen")]

iPadOS 17 allows users to personalize their Lock Screens with widgets and fonts. Interactive widgets can be placed on both the Lock Screen and Home Screen for quick access to customizable information, such as weather and reminders.[[21]](#cite_note-21)

#### Health

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=IPadOS&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Health")]

The introduction of the Health app on iPad provides a central location to view and manage health data.

#### Messages and FaceTime

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=IPadOS&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Messages and FaceTime")]

New communication features for Messages and FaceTime are introduced, such as audio/voice messages (like a voicemail) if your call is not answered on FaceTime, and using your Apple TV for FaceTime calls with your iPad acting as a camera. Messages introduces the combining of search filters and content types when searching through your messages, as well as transcriptions for audio messages.[[22]](#cite_note-22)

#### Accessibility

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=IPadOS&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Accessibility")]

New accessibility features like Screen Distance and improved Voice Control expand usability options for a wider range of users.

#### App Updates

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=IPadOS&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: App Updates")]

Several core apps receive updates, including Photos, Safari, Notes, and Reminders. These updates bring new functionality and improvements to enhance the overall iPad experience.[[23]](#cite_note-23)

### iPadOS 18

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=IPadOS&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: iPadOS 18")]

Further information: [iPadOS 18 § Features](/wiki/IPadOS_18#Features "IPadOS 18")

### iPadOS 26

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=IPadOS&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: iPadOS 26")]

Further information: [iPadOS 26 § New features and changes](/wiki/IPadOS_26#New_features_and_changes "IPadOS 26")

iPadOS 26 has a new [Liquid Glass](/wiki/Liquid_Glass "Liquid Glass") design, new apps, and more.
